channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Authorities continue investigating unusual drone activity around Trump's golf club in New Jersey: video
item.description: Officials are still investigating unusual drone activity reported in recent weeks in New Jersey. The FAA set temporary restrictions above Trump National Golf Club in Bedminster.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Police hunt for UnitedHealthcare CEO's masked killer after 'brazen, targeted' attack on NYC street
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A gunman killed UnitedHealthcare’s CEO on Wednesday in a “brazen, targeted attack” outside a Manhattan hotel where the health insurer was holding its investor conference, police said, setting off a massive search for the fleeing assailant hours before the annual Rockefeller Center Ch
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, KAREN MATTHEWS and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Two children, ages 5 and 6, wounded and gunman dead after shooting at Northern California school
item.description: PALERMO, Calif. (AP) — Two children were wounded Wednesday in a shooting at a tiny religious K-8 school in Northern California and deputies arrived to find the gunman on the ground near the playground, dead after apparently shooting himself, sheriff’s officials said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Hegseth nomination sinking fast in the Senate
item.description: Senate Republicans say Pete Hegseth, President-elect Trump’s choice to head the Defense Department, faces a very tough path to confirmation in the Senate and his bleak prospects have been communicated directly to the Trump transition team, which is now mulling other options.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Underground voids from abandoned mines pose danger to people and property when land collapses
item.description: Swaths of Pennsylvania and many other states are honeycombed with old, unstable mines that can cause the earth to suddenly give way and threaten people and property.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL RUBINKAM and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Search intensifies for missing Pennsylvania grandmother as crews encounter unstable mine shafts
item.description: The search for Pennsylvania grandmother Elizabeth Pollard continued on Wednesday. Pollard, 64, is believed to have fallen into a sinkhole on Dec. 2 while searching for her pet.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Is it hotter than the sun outside? Wacky weather app bug says it is
item.description: If you took a look at the weather widget on your Windows computer Wednesday afternoon, you may have noticed that the numbers were higher than normal for winter.
item.dc:creator: Joey Rather, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump's picks for key positions in his second administration
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has filled the key posts for his second term in office, prioritizing loyalty to him after he felt bruised and hampered by internal squabbling during his first term.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT and DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Two children, ages 5 and 6, wounded in shooting at Northern California school
item.description: A suspected gunman was killed after a shooting at Feather River School of Seventh-Day Adventists in Palermo, Butte County.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Memphis police use excessive force and discriminate against Black people, Justice Department finds
item.description: The Memphis Police Department uses excessive force and discriminates against Black people, according to the findings of a U.S. Department of Justice investigation launched after the beating death of Tyre Nichols after a traffic stop in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Sainz And Jonathan Mattise, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Searchers don't expect to find woman in sinkhole alive: Pennsylvania police
item.description: “We’ve had no signs of any form of life or anything,” Pennsylvania State Police spokesperson Trooper Steve Limani said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump changes White House counsel pick, tapping David Warrington
item.description: Trump in a statement said Warrington would serve as the top attorney in the White House and as assistant to the president.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: New LAPD chief says he will work to protect immigrants ahead of Trump's plans for mass deportations
item.description: The new Los Angeles police chief says in an interview with The Associated Press that his department is working with consulate offices for Mexico and other Central American countries to draft a plan to protect immigrants ahead of the Trump administration’s plans for mass deportations.
item.dc:creator: Jaimie Ding, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: At least two Illinois schools are without heat; parents protest
item.description: "It's the superintendent that needs to make the call. He needs to cancel school until we get these buildings under control and the schools are safe," parent Sequoia Williams said.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court Latest: Majority of justices appear likely to uphold state ban on transgender care
item.description: The Supreme Court heard arguments on Wednesday in just its second major transgender rights case, a challenge to a Tennessee law that bans gender-affirming care for minors.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A list of mispronounced words provides a retrospective of 2024, from Kamala to Chappell
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris and breakout pop star Chappell Roan were among the year's most talked-about people. Their names were also among the most mispronounced.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Daily walks can add years to your life, says new study
item.description: Check out fitness trackers, running shoes, workout clothes and more to make your fitness journey as easy and comfortable as it can possibly be.
item.dc:creator: Christina Marfice, Fox 5

item.title: Albuquerque dog undergoes leg amputation surgery after being shot in drive-by
item.description: Halo, a 10-year-old American pit bull mix, was struck by a bullet on Nov. 3 while at home in the middle of the night.
item.dc:creator: Fallon Fischer, NewsNation

item.title: Trump’s pick to run the DEA withdraws his name from consideration. Trump says he pulled the plug
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Chad Chronister, Donald Trump's pick to run the Drug Enforcement Administration, has withdrawn his name from consideration for the job, becoming the second person selected by the president-elect to bow out quickly after being selected for a position requiring Senate confirmation.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mother sentenced in 8-month-old son's bathtub drowning
item.description: A Sparta Township mother will spend months in jail in the bathtub drowning death of her 8-month-old son last fall.
item.dc:creator: Katie Rosendale, NewsNation

item.title: Harris found success with women who have cats, but Trump got the dog owner vote: AP VoteCast
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The lead-up to the 2024 election was all about cat owners. But in the end, the dogs had their day.
item.dc:creator: LINLEY SANDERS, HUMERA LODHI and ANNIE NG, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Community 'college deserts' leave students stranded from higher education
item.description: College deserts have gained more attention in recent years as they are considered a significant reason some groups are not attending college.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KRON4

item.title: British indie rock band robbed at gunpoint in California '10 minutes into US tour,' group says
item.description: Sports Team, a six-piece London-based alternative rock band, had stopped for coffee when someone alerted them that their van was being ransacked by robbers, according to their latest Instagram post.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Police say searchers don’t expect to find woman in Pennsylvania sinkhole alive
item.description: The search for a woman who is believed to have fallen into a sinkhole in western Pennsylvania has become a recovery effort after two treacherous days of digging through mud and rock produced no signs of life.
item.dc:creator: Mark Scolforo, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Students at Ohio elementary school can participate in Satanic Temple's religious learning program
item.description: The Satanic Temple is offering religious learning instruction to students at an Ohio elementary school as part of the state's religious release program.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lead Daniel Penny prosecutor secured light sentence for thug who killed 87-year-old in ATM robbery
item.description: Dafna Yoran, the lead prosecutor in the subway chokehold case against Daniel Penny, once pushed for a lenient "restorative justice" penalty for a Manhattan killer.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: App claims its AI can predict when you'll die
item.description: The app also features a countdown clock that features your death day and a picture of the Grim Reaper.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, KRON4

item.title: US border arrests drop 17% in November, dousing predictions of post-election surge
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — U.S. authorities made about 46,700 arrests for illegally crossing the border from Mexico in November, down about 17% from October to a new low for Joe Biden's presidency, an official said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Prosecutors: DC police officer’s talk with Proud Boys leader grew secretive as arrest neared
item.description: Metropolitan Police Department Lt. Shane Lamond’s communication with the former leader of the Proud Boys increased and grew more secretive as pressure to arrest him mounted in 2020, prosecutors said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9

item.title: Global warming fills New England's rich waters with death traps for endangered sea turtles
item.description: As global warming fills the plankton-rich waters of New England with death traps for sea turtles, the number of stranded reptiles has multiplied over the last 20 years, filling one specialized animal hospital with the endangered creatures.
item.dc:creator: RODRIQUE NGOWI and PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Sinkhole may have swallowed Pennsylvania grandmother looking for missing cat: police
item.description: Authorities fear a Pennsylvania grandmother searching for her missing cat may have been swallowed by a sinkhole.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, KRON4

item.title: Snowstorm causes massive 17-vehicle crash in Michigan
item.description: HARTFORD, Mich. (WFLA) — A massive crash in Michigan left a person in a hospital with critical injuries after 17 vehicles collided Monday. According to the Michigan State Police, the crash occurred on I-94 near Hartford during a lake-effect snowstorm that persisted into Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: We're all saying Daniel Craig's name wrong
item.description: His name may no longer be "Bond, James Bond," but former 007 Daniel Craig is setting the record straight about how to pronounce his name.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, KTLA 5

item.title: US biathletes fear retaliation for speaking out about sexual harassment, report finds
item.description: U.S. biathletes face “misogynistic” behavior while racing and fear retaliation if they report their concerns, according to an inquiry by an independent contractor hired to assess athlete safety. The investigation of U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FBI offers $15K reward in case of Maine teen missing since September
item.description: The teenager reportedly had an argument with her sister before walking out of her house into the woods.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, KRON4

item.title: Law enforcement leaders react to Trump selecting Hillsborough Sheriff Chad Chronister as DEA Administrator
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump announced Saturday afternoon he's nominating Hillsborough County Sheriff Chad Chronister to be the next Administrator of the Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA).
item.dc:creator: Melissa Marino, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Harvey Weinstein hospitalized after 'alarming blood test,' attorney says
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Harvey Weinstein was hospitalized Monday following an “alarming blood test,” his attorney said, less than a week after the disgraced movie mogul filed a legal claim alleging substandard medical care at New York City's notorious jail complex.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4
